purpose: Phase 1 H19 validation pilot (reps=2) — does a frontier agent equipped with the import-edge-improved codeweb out-discover grep on Go+Rust targets

gorilla-newrouter.note: grep SATURATES — distinctive name, every caller writes NewRouter(; no headroom for codeweb

ripgrep-escape.note: codeweb's cross-file use-callers helped marginally but grep 'escape(' also found most; within noise at n=2

verdict: NULL on these targets, but a VALID, useful null: (1) harness behaved correctly (integrity clean, grading correct, treatment used the new engine and recovered the Rust cross-file callers); (2) codeweb never REGRESSED recall (treatment recall >= control on both); (3) the null is a TARGET-SELECTION artifact — both targets have distinctive names where grep already saturates. The prior JS pilot's win came from grep-HOSTILE targets (common names like 'merge', import/test-mediated callers like AxiosError's 76 deps). H19 generalization must be tested on grep-hostile Go/Rust targets, not distinctive-name ones. Do NOT scale these targets to 8 reps — it would only confirm a null. Validate-small worked exactly as intended.
